title: x86-64
content:
Registers# General Purpose# Register 64 32 16 8 accumulator %rax %eax %ax %al base %rbx %ebx %bx %bl counter %rcx %ecx %cx %cl data %rdx %edx %dx %dl source index %rsi %esi %si %sil dest index %rdi %edi %di %dil stack pointer %rsp — — — base pointer %rbp — — — extended %r8–%r15 %r8d %r8w %r8b Size Suffixes (GNU)# q — 8 bytes l — 4 bytes w — 2 bytes b — 1 byte ABI — System V (Linux)# Function Parameters# movq $1, %rdi movq $2, %rsi movq $3, %rdx movq $4, %rcx movq $5, %r8 movq $6, %r9 pushq $10 pushq $9 pushq $8 pushq $7 call myfuncReturn Values# %rax # primary %rdx # secondary (rare)Caller-Saved (volatile — assume clobbered after call)# %rax %rcx %rdx %rsi %rdi %r8 %r9 %r10 %r11
Callee-Saved (must preserve if used)# %rbx %rbp %r12 %r13 %r14 %r15
Syscalls# %rax # syscall number %rdi # param 1 %rsi # param 2 %rdx # param 3 %r10 # param 4 %r8 # param 5 %r9 # param 6 syscall Stack Frame# start of a function:
pushq %rbp # save the pointer to the previous stack frame movq %rsp, %rbp # copy the stack pointer to the base pointer for a fixed reference point subq $16, %rsp # reserve memory on the stack (16-byte aligned) # or enter $16, $0 # second arg always 0 (enter is slower than manual)end of a function:
movq %rbp, %rsp # restore the stack pointer popq %rbp # restore the base pointer ret # or leave # (leave is faster than manual) retLocal Variables# local vars are negative offsets from %rbp .equ LOCAL_FOO, -8 .equ LOCAL_BAR, -16 enter $16, $0 movq %rax, LOCAL_FOO(%rbp) # write movq LOCAL_FOO(%rbp), %rax # read if all work fits in registers and no callee-saved regs touched → skip frame setup entirely call / ret# call myfunc # pushes return address onto stack, jumps to myfunc ret # pops return address, jumps to it
